name: oats/AGENT.python.tools.md

oats/agent_get_tool_choices_for_prompt.p.get_tool_choices_for_prompt: get tool choices for a prompt using the OAT index with multi-tool resolution
oats/agent_get_tool_choices_for_prompt.p.get_prompt_choices: get prompt choices from the OAT config using the get_prompt_choices method
oats/agent_get_tool_choices_for_prompt.p.get_best_matches_bm25: get the best BM25 matches for a prompt when prompt choices are unavailable
oats/agent_get_tool_choices_for_prompt.p.run_agent_get_tool_choices_cli: run the CLI tool to get prompt choices from the OAT index with a prompt argument
oats/agent_get_tool_choices_for_prompt.p.parse_args: parse command line arguments for the OAT prompt choices CLI including prompt top-k and verbose

oats/call_tool_with_loader1.py.create_LocalTool: create a LocalTool instance and set its name, description, parameters, and implementation callable
oats/call_tool_with_loader1.py.run_LocalTool_execute: run the LocalTool execute method with args and a ToolContext to get a ToolResult
oats/call_tool_with_loader1.py.load_tools_from_repo_uses_index: load tools from a source index file matched against a prompt and wrap them as LocalTool instances
oats/call_tool_with_loader1.py.run_tool_call: run an LLM tool-calling loop using LiteLLM with dynamically loaded tool implementations and a user prompt
oats/call_tool_with_loader1.py.run_main_cli: run the CLI to load tools from a repo index by prompt and execute the first matched tool

oats/date.py.get_third_friday_dates: generate the third Friday dates for the next 6 months in YYYYMMDD format
oats/date.py.utc: get the current UTC datetime as a timezone-aware datetime object
oats/date.py.naive: get the current UTC datetime as a timezone-naive datetime object
oats/date.py.get_utc_str: get the current UTC datetime formatted as a YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS string
oats/date.py.review_date_module: review the date module functions for UTC and naive datetime utilities

oats/determine_best_tools1.py.determine_best_tools: find the best tool matches from a uses schema using BM25, TF-IDF, or embeddings
oats/determine_best_tools1.py.rank_with_bm25: rank corpus documents against a query using the BM25Okapi algorithm
oats/determine_best_tools1.py.rank_with_tfidf: rank corpus documents against a query using TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity
oats/determine_best_tools1.py.rank_with_embeddings: rank corpus documents against a query using sentence transformer embeddings and cosine similarity
oats/determine_best_tools1.py.rerank: rerank retrieval results using a cross-encoder model for improved semantic accuracy

oats/get_oat_config.py.get_oat_config: get the singleton OatConfig instance that holds repo uses index and data settings

oats/load_tools_from_source1.py.run_load_tools: load public functions from python files into OpenAI tool schemas and callable implementations
oats/load_tools_from_source1.py.run_run_tool_call: run a tool call loop that sends a prompt to an LLM and executes returned tool calls
oats/load_tools_from_source1.py.run_get_best_tools_for_prompt: determine the best tool source files for a prompt using BM25 retrieval and OAT
oats/load_tools_from_source1.py.run_run_tool_for_prompt: determine best tools for a prompt, load them, and execute the full tool call loop
oats/load_tools_from_source1.py.run_main_cli: run the FunctionGemma CLI to dynamically load tool files and execute tool calls for a prompt

oats/log.py.create_logger_instance: create a logger Lg instance with a custom name and optional color support
oats/log.py.log_messages_with_levels: log messages at different levels like info, debug, warning, error, and critical
oats/log.py.save_logs_to_file: save collected log messages to a file with optional append mode and length limits
oats/log.py.log_markdown_headers: log markdown header strings that get rendered with rich Markdown formatting and colored level tags
oats/log.py.get_logger_logs: retrieve the list of all logged messages from the logger instance

oats/models.py.create_OatPromptChoices: create an OatPromptChoices model to store matched actions prompts and source file results
oats/models.py.create_OatConfig: create an OatConfig instance that loads and indexes a repo uses JSON file
oats/models.py.get_prompt_choices: get prompt choices by matching a user prompt prefix against indexed repo actions
oats/models.py.get_best_matches_bm25: get the best matching actions for a prompt using BM25Okapi scoring and top-k retrieval
oats/models.py.review_OatConfig_get_prompt_choices: review the OatConfig get_prompt_choices method to understand prefix matching and tool result scoring

oats/pp.py.pp_dict: use pp to pretty print a Python dictionary as indented JSON
oats/pp.py.pp_list: use pp to pretty print a Python list as indented JSON
oats/pp.py.pp_any: use pp to pretty print any Python object as indented JSON
oats/pp.py.pp_none: use pp with no arguments to return an empty JSON object
oats/pp.py.pp_escape_slashes: use pp to pretty print JSON with forward slashes unescaped
